collection.title: Last Chance
collection.description:
Last Chance is your final opportunity to place a bid.
This collection features auctions ending in less than 30 minutes.
Time is running out — act now or let someone else take the win.

collection_translations.title: Última oportunidad
collection_translations.description:
Última oportunidad es tu última oportunidad para pujar.
Esta colección incluye subastas que terminan en menos de 30 minutos.
El tiempo se acaba: ¡actúa ahora o deja que otro gane!

collection_translations.title: Dernière Chance
collection_translations.description:
Dernière Chance est votre dernière occasion de placer une enchère.

Cette collection présente des enchères se terminant en moins de 30 minutes.

Le temps presse — agissez maintenant ou laissez quelqu'un d'autre remporter la victoire.

collection_translations.title: الأخيرة فرصة
collection_translations.description: Last Chance هي فرصتك الأخيرة لتقديم عرض مالي. ويضم هذا المجموعة مزادات تنتهي في أقل من 30 دقيقة. والمجال يزول - فاقم الآن أو اترك شخص آخر يفوز.

collection_translations.title: Sista Chansen
collection_translations.description:
Last Chance är din sista chans att lägga in en budgivning.

Denna samling innehåller auktioner som avslutas på mindre än 30 minuter.

Tiden är ute — handla nu eller låt någon annan vinna.

collection_translations.title: Letzte Chance
collection_translations.description:
Die letzte Chance ist Ihre letzte Gelegenheit, einen Gebot abzugeben.

Diese Sammlung umfasst Auktionen, die in weniger als 30 Minuten enden.

Die Zeit läuft ab – handeln Sie jetzt oder lassen Sie jemand anderen den Gewinn erringen.

collection_translations.title: Laatste Kans
collection_translations.description:
Laatste kans is uw laatste gelegenheid om een bod te plaatsen.

Deze collectie omvat veilingen die binnen minder dan 30 minuten eindigen.

De tijd tikt weg — neem nu actie of laat alstublieft iemand anders de winst behalen.

collection_translations.title: Poslední Šance
collection_translations.description:
Poslední šance je vaše poslední příležitost k podání nabídky.

Tato sbírka obsahuje aukce končící v méně než 30 minutách.

Čas běží – jednáte teď nebo necháte někdo jiného získat vítězství.

collection_translations.title: 最后机会
collection_translations.description:
Last Chance 是您最后一次机会提交投标。

本次拍卖会特征的是拍卖会截止时间在 30 分钟以内。

时间正在流逝 —— 立刻行动，否则会让别人赢得胜利。

collection_translations.title: Ultima Occasione
collection_translations.description:
Ultimo Chiamato è la tua ultima occasione per effettuare una offerta.

Questa collezione comprende le aste che si concludono in meno di 30 minuti.

Il tempo sta scadendo — agisci subito o lascia che qualcun altro vincia.

collection_translations.title: Oportunitate Ultimă
collection_translations.description:
Ultima Oportunitate este ultima ta șansă de a pune o ofertă.

Acest colecție prezintă licitații care se încheie în mai puțin de 30 de minute.

Se scurge timpul — acționează acum sau lasă ca altcineva să câștige.

collection_translations.title: Son Şans
collection_translations.description:
Son Şans, bir teklifinizi koymanıza kalan son fırsattır.

Bu koleksiyon, 30 dakika içinde sona erecek teklifleri içerir.

Zaman bitiyor — şimdi harekete geçin veya başka biri kazanmaya hak kazansın.

collection_translations.title: Última Oportunidade
collection_translations.description:
Última Oportunidade é sua última chance de fazer uma oferta.

Esta coleção apresenta leilões que terminam em menos de 30 minutos.

O tempo está correndo — agir agora ou deixe que alguém mais leve a vitória.

collection_translations.title: Ostatnia Szansa
collection_translations.description:
Ostatnia szansa to Twoja ostatnia możliwość złożenia oferty.

Ta kolekcja obejmuje aukcje kończące się w mniej niż 30 minut.

Czas biegnie w przeciwnym kierunku — działaj teraz, czy pozostawić zwycięstwo kogoś innemu.

collection_translations.title: Sidste Chance
collection_translations.description:
Den sidste mulighed er din sidste chance for at placere en budgivning.

Denne samling indeholder auktioner der afslutter på mindre end 30 minutter.

Tiden løber ud – handling nu eller lad nogen anden vinde.

collection_translations.title: Siste Mulighet
collection_translations.description:
Siste mulighet er din siste mulighet til å plassere en budgivning.
Denne samlingen består av auksjoner som slutter i mindre enn 30 minutter.
Tiden løper ut - handling nå eller lad noen annen vinne.

lots.title: Glenn Brown: Art Edition No. 13–112 Sizewell A
lots.description:
This Art Edition book treats the viewer to a close encounter with Glenn Brown’s paintings, paint sculptures, and drawings, eye-deceiving renderings of brushstrokes from sources between sci-fi and the old masters.

  • Bice Curiger (Swiss).
  • Dr John Paul Stonard (English).
  • John Chilver (English).
  • Hans Werner Holzwarth (German).
  • Köln: TASCHEN, 2023.
  • 474 pages.
  • Hardcover in clamshell box.
  • Part of a numbered edition of 100 (No. 13–112) signed by Glenn Brown.
  • Accompanied by a three color woodblock and lithographic plate print Sizewell A, 2023, also signed by the artist.


The main spirit that runs through Glenn Brown’s work is transformation. The motif may come from old master paintings, the color scheme from modernist ones, the mood might be set by a title that quotes from a pop song. Brown became famous (and sometimes infamous) for his unabashedly appropriative work in the early 1990s, a golden era for young British art. He rendered the thick brushstrokes of expressionist painters like Frank Auerbach or Karel Appel almost photo-realistically, carefully modeling their dashing gestures with a fine brush until his smooth surfaces deceived the viewer’s eye. Taking space fantasies from science-fiction paperbacks, he made epic history paintings out of tiny book illustrations. Brown turned to the old masters, updating their historical models with a contemporary understanding of paint’s materiality and a barbed sense of color.


Following the artist’s development through the decades, we find his engagement with the medium deepening and branching out. Brown invents his own brushstrokes that take over the picture’s subject, unsettling viewers by their sickly hues. When he starts making sculptures from paint, he absurdly layers brushstrokes on top of each other until they become three-dimensional figures, sometimes growing from bases of historical bronze statuettes. Then he arranges his works in installations as an interplay of different media, or exhibits them in the collection of an art museum in a dialogue between his own paintings and the works that inspired them. In the mid-2010s, Brown stopped painting altogether to explore the medium of drawing, often creating work that reacts to vintage wooden frames he is collecting. Upon his return to painting, there is a newfound freedom of lines dancing and mingling, colors radiating from the core of Janus-like superimpositions of two counteracting motifs.


This book has been conceived and realized in close collaboration with the artist, curating an overview of his work in unprecedented depth. All authors have worked with Brown before to now deliver an incisive portrait of the artist, an art-historical survey of his work and development, as well as essays on different artistic approaches and genres. Comments written by Brown especially for this edition discuss specific works and artistic choices. More than anything, it is the book’s large format that makes the eye-deceivingly smooth brushwork and exuberant lines come alive, allowing viewers to immerse themselves in every detail and share the artist’s contagious love of painting and paint.

All Lots are offered for sale in the condition they are in at the time of sale and sold “AS IS” in accordance with the Conditions of Business for Buyers. Prospective buyers should inspect each Lot to satisfy themselves as to condition. The images provided here are provided to assist you with assessing the condition of each Lot and are for guidance only; they should not be considered a replacement for inspecting the Lot in person.


Buyers are encouraged to collect their purchased Lots in person at our New York premises. Please note we do not ship spirits to any location outside of Manhattan.


﻿Please note that all spirits are sold for collection in New York or delivery within New York via specialized carriers. For delivery outside of the United States, please see Important Information for Purchases at Wine and Spirit Sales in the Conditions of Business.


Please note that for Wine and Spirits Lots, the Buyer’s Premium rate is 24% of the Hammer Price and the Overhead Premium rate is 1% of the Hammer Price. The Buyer’s Premium and Overhead Premium are subject to any applicable VAT/GST and/or sales or use tax. Please refer to the Conditions of Business for further details.

lots.title: Adventures of Huckleberry Finn Illustration, Signed
lots.description:
An illustration from Mark Twain's classic novel The Adventures Of Huckleberry Finn, which has been signed by the artist.

  • Worth Brehm (American, 1883-1928).
  • Signed on the lower left.
  • Charcoal on artist's board.
  • This work is presented matted and framed.


A poignant depiction of the scene when Huck, out hunting on Jackson's Island, meets the runaway slave Jim.


Huck stands at the left of the drawing, while Jim, at the foot of a large tree, kneels in supplication. The drama of the moment is enhanced by the artist's use of light, which, coming from behind Huck, falls on the tree, bringing the figure of Jim into sharp relief.

lots.title: The Bridge of San Luis Rey First Edition | With Signed Letter
lots.description:
A first edition, first printing of The Bridge of San Luis Rey by Thornton Wilder, accompanied by a handwritten and signed letter discussing the novel.

  • Thornton Wilder (American).
  • London: Longmans, 1927.
  • In an unclipped wrapper.


Within, a page long hand-written letter SIGNED by Wilder is affixed and unfurls from the front pastedown and is dated October 9th 1934 (originating from The University of Chicago). The letter discusses the letters of Marquesa de Montemayor and states her character is based on ‘that of Mme de Sevigne’. A marvelous association to the novel.


A bridge in Peru collapses, killing five people. Brother Juniper investigates, seeking divine meaning in the tragedy. One victim is the Marquesa de Montemayor, a wealthy, lonely woman estranged from her daughter. Known for her eloquent letters, she transforms from bitterness to compassion through her unrequited love. Her death, along with the others, forms part of Wilder’s meditation on fate, love, and human connection. The novel ultimately suggests that love endures beyond life’s seeming randomness, giving meaning where reason fails.

Wrapper with a chip to the head of the spine and to the edges.

The spine is browned.

lots.title: Signed Letter from Sylvia Plath to her Mother
lots.description:
A signed letter from Sylvia Plath to her mother.

  • Sylvia Plath (American).
  • One leaf, recto and verso, on light blue Smith College stationery.
  • Framed with contemporaneous black-and-white photograph of Plath.


Sylvia Plath (1932–63) matriculated at Smith College in the fall of 1950, and quickly formed a tight bond with her roommate, Marcia Brown (1932–2012). In the winter of 1951, Plath and Brown visited the latter's maternal aunt, Harriet Bosworth, in Francestown, New Hampshire, a trip that clearly enthralled Plath, who writes in the first paragraph, "It is now midnight Sunday night and I have gotten back from the nicest weekend I ever had!" She continues:


"Marcia and I got up at 10 and had a lovely liesurely [sic] breakfast in the front living room of the dear little house. Then we went out into the most beautiful world imagineable [sic]! Snow had fallen in a fine powder last night, and the sun was out in a snow-blue sky. A white-New-England church is so lovely — and it was one of those heavenly dry-cold days, with blinding sun and snow and sharp blue shadows. The air was swimmingly blue."


Plath goes on to recount skiing for the first time — "I have never been so thrilled in my life!" — and while noting "the modest little gently rolling hill… was not Sun Valley," stated that, "Skiing, if you can do it well, must be pretty close to feeling like God." She closes with, "to bed & my six hour day tomorrow — your happy girl, Sylvia."


Plath and Brown maintained their close friendship until Sylvia's suicide in 1963. Brown is mentioned multiple times in Plath's journals, and the poet sent her more than 20 letters, all of which are currently housed in the Sylvia Plath Collection at Smith. Plath also incorporated Brown into two poems, the first being "The Babysitters" (1961), which recounts a summer both girls worked as nannies in Swampscott, Massachusetts, and the second an unpublished pieced titled "Marcia," in which Plath writes, "she will be always as now: cheeks appleshining translucent from soapandwater scrubbing. she will be always sight of market in the morning: red cheek of beet and radish squashyellow hair and mirrored purpleblack of eggplant eye laced with celerysilver lashes, vegetable and vital."

lots.title: Datejust Reference 116300, a stainless steel automatic wristwatch with date, Circa 2016
lots.description:
The DateJust is a significant model that has been in Rolex’s collection for over 40 years. From being housed in a cushion-shaped case to a waterproof oyster case, and being crowned to the very first automatic wristwatch with an automatically changing date window, the DateJust is truly an exemplary representation of Rolex’s history in great watchmaking.


The reference 116300 belonging to the DateJust II collection is housed in a 41 mm contemporary case that is extraordinarily versatile for both casual and professional attire. Furthermore, the present watch has been sent for polishing to remove the mirror finishing and granting the watch an elegant and subtle matte finish.

This tribute to Antoine de Saint-Exupéry features IWC’s signature annual calendar complication and a red gold rotor depicting the Little Prince standing on his asteroid, visible through the sapphire case back - a poetic fusion of aviation, literature, and horological artistry.


Includes presentation non-IWC box.

The watch is in overall very good condition and presents beautifully with no obvious surface marks visible to the unaided eye. Strap shows signs of wear. Prior service history is unknown. The movement is running at the time of cataloging, however, it was not tested for the accuracy of time or duration of the power reserve and may need service at the buyer's discretion. A Martian condemned to death is permitted instead to "fly" into space. This is achieved by him attaching a wire to the edge of a hole running through the center of Mars and then, fixed to the wire, falling through the hole down to earth. After going through some adventures, recounted in a primitive style, Edison helps him return.

lots.title: French James Bond From Russia With Love Movie Poster
lots.description:
A movie poster for the James Bond film From Russia With Love.

  • Boris Grinsson (Russian, 1907-1999).
  • United Artists.
  • Linen backed.
  • This work is presented unframed.


In Sean Connery’s second outing as Bond, James Bond he willingly falls into an assassination plot involving a naive Russian beauty in order to retrieve a Soviet encryption device that was stolen by S.P.E.C.T.R.E. From Russia With Love remains on of the very best of the James Bond franchise.


Boris Grinsson (1907 - 1999) was a renowned Russian born French artist famous for drawing the designs for over 2000 French film posters for American, Italian, and French films during a career spanning forty years (from the 1930s to the 1970s). He produced a vast body of work in a variety of genres, including This Gun for Hire (1942), Gilda (1946), The Lady from Shanghai (1948) and From Russia with Love (1963).

Original folded as issued now professionally linen backed.

Just very light touch top to folds.

Minor signs of age and handling.

Poster will be sent rolled.

lots.title: Cathay: Poems After Li Po
lots.description:
Signed limited edition of Pound's Cathay with seven color woodcuts by Francesco Clemente.

  • Ezra Pound (French).
  • Woodcuts by Francesco Clemente (Italian).
  • Designed by Dan Carr (American).
  • [New York]: The Limited Editions Club, [1993].
  • Large octavo.
  • Printed by Arthur Larson at Horton Tank Graphics on handmade Japanese Ogawashi paper.
  • Edition limited to 300 numbered copies signed by Clemente.
  • Original pale blue Japanese linen, original slipcase.


"This volume is all the more remarkable for the fact that Pound knew no Chinese. Yet these translations of eighteen medieval poems, mainly by Li Po (A.D. 70I-762), are acknowledged masterpieces. Pound worked from notes by Ernest Fenollosa, a great American scholar of Oriental literature and art. These notes Pound turned to poetry, imbued with the scent and flavor of their Chinese originals. Italian‑born artist Francesco Clemente, as well, feels the magnetic pull of the East. There have been scores of books and articles by and about Clemente; and exhibitions of his art all over the world, including a large 1990 show at the Philadelphia Museum of Art. As the organizer of the Philadelphia exhibit wrote, Clemente’s art 'resonates between intensities of the cerebral and the sensual.' Especially here, in these seven exquisite color woodcuts" (Publisher).

lots.title: New York Beat: Jean-Michel Basquiat Downtown 81
lots.description:
Inscribed by early Basquiat champion & historic downtown art scene critic and curator, Glenn O’Brien, New York Beat brilliantly documents the production of Downtown 81, director Edo Bertoglio's seminal take of New York's Downtown scene, featuring a turn, 19 year old Jean-Michel Basquiat as semi-biographical protagonist.

  • Glenn O'Brien (American).
  • Photographs by Bertoglio and Maripol.
  • Tokyo: Petit Grand Publishing, Inc., 2001.
  • 112 pages.
  • Hardcover book with dust wrapper.
  • Reverse of interior jacket forms a striking vertical poster featuring Basquiat's portrait.
  • Text in English and Japanese.


"Shot as New York Beat in 1980-1981 but completed for release only last year, Downtown 81 emerges as a nostalgic portrait of pre-Giuliani Manhattan, an unruly place full of garbage, graffiti, rubble-strewn lots, unlicensed after-hours clubs and highly idealistic kids eager to make their mark as avant-garde artists and musicians.


The film's central figure is Jean, a young Bohemian engagingly played by a not yet famous Jean-Michel Basquiat.


Jean survives mainly by relying on the worldwide network of hip: there will always be another bar to go to, another gig to Crash, another wealthy European to write a check, another gorgeous model in a Cadillac convertible to give him a ride home.


The script, by the rock critic Glenn O'Brien, also the film's co-producer, follows the basic day-in-the-life format, beginning with Jean's release from a hospital.


Jean returns to his Alphabet City tenement to find an angry landlord demanding his back rent, and departs with a painting he hopes to sell. The day brings many disappointments but also many fortuitous encounters. He meets assorted new-wave musicians (Kid Creole and the Coconuts, Tuxedomoon, James White and the Blacks, DNA, the Plastics, and Walter Steding, most of whom perform); go-go dancers; and street people, among them Deborah Harry, who plays a bag lady with magical powers.


It's as a documentary that Downtown 81 is most successful, particularly at those moments when the somewhat unfocused filmmaking allows us to look past the foreground characters and catch glimpses of a vanished cityscape: the pornography shops and grind houses of West 42nd Street, the greasy spoons and outlet stores of East 14th Street, the labyrinthine interior of the Mudd Club in what was then the largely uncharted territory south of Canal Street." (New York Times, 7/13/01)


Glenn O'Brien, an American writer and art/fashion critic, was a close friend, confidante and early champion of Jean-Michel Basquiat.

lots.title: Snow White Caldecott Medal Honor Book Illustration Art
lots.description:
Wanda Gág study of the Brothers Grimm classic, Snow White.

  • Wanda Gág (American, 1893-1946).
  • Original art depicting Snow White and Prince Charming.
  • Signed by Gág.
  • Single study archivally matted, framed, and glazed.


Wonderful signed and finished pen-and-ink study for the illustration appearing in Gág's Caldecott Medal Honor Book, Snow White And The Seven Dwarfs.


This illustration appears on page 39 of the first edition, published by Coward McCann in 1938. Snow White has fallen asleep after eating the poisoned apple and lies under glass cover in the woods. Prince Charming is approaching on the left, a dwarf sleeps in a tree to the right, and forest creatures watch in anticipation. A wonderful image; original art by Gág is quite rare.

lots.title: A Second Voyage Round The World
lots.description:
A first edition copy of A Second Voyage Round The World.

  • James Cook (English).
  • A Second Voyage round the world, in the Years MDCCLXXII, LXXIII, LXXIV, LXXV. By James Cook, Esq. Commander of His Majesty’s Bark, the Resolution. Undertaken by Order of the King, and encouraged by a Parliamentary Grant of Four Thousand Pounds. Drawn up from Authentic Papers.
  • London: Printed for the Editor: Sold by J. Almon, opposite Burlington-House, Picadilly; And Fletcher & Hodson, Cambridge, 1776.
  • Quarto.
  • 102 pages.
  • Pages uncut in original boards, neatly rebacked to style.
  • Presented in a modern blue cloth box, red morocco label.


The rarest of all the surreptitious accounts of Cook’s voyages, issued with a title phrased to mislead the public into believing it to be Cook’s own account, it was initially accepted as an authentic account but the book was quickly exposed and denounced.


Following the return of the Third Voyage with news of Cook’s death in 1780 and the subsequent heightened public interest in Cook, the original title-leaf was cancelled and replaced by a rephrased title-page with a new date, now attributing authorship to ‘An Officer on Board’. This ploy seems also to have been unsuccessful – the public was interested in the Third Voyage – and, consequently, that second issue is as rare as the first.


Beaglehole remarks that ’it recounts a few incidents, not otherwise known, which do not seem out of key with the voyage as a whole’, but goes on to say that ‘the rest is so palpably fake, and in the most sensational terms, that it must be regarded as original invention – on the basis, perhaps, of a reading of Marra or conversation with a stray sailor...’. Georg Forster declared that it was the work of a Cambridge undergraduate and subsequent opinion has tended to echo contemporary sentiment. Nevertheless, Renard ventures that the account derives ‘from the journal of one of the officers on board’, while it has even been suggested that the journal of Lieutenant Pickersgill may have been the source.

lots.title: The Pinch Hitter / Bollklubbens Mascot Swedish Movie Poster
lots.description:

  • Eric Rohman (Swedish, 1891-1949).
  • Linen backed and conservation framed in obeche wood, card stock and UV plexiglass.


Eric Rohman was in large part responsible for the development of a distinctive style in Swedish posters during the first decades of the twentieth century. This poster was in The Julius Paul Collection, which formed part of The Albertina Museum Collection, Vienna, Austria.

  • Signed Graff
  • 18 karat white gold
  • Circa 2000s
  • Gross weight approximately 15.82 dwts
  • Length 17 inches

lots.title: Ressence Type 8C 'Cobalt Blue'. A titanium automatic wristwatch. Circa 2022
lots.description:
The Ressence Type 8 Cobalt Blue is a minimalist luxury timepiece distinguished by its ultra-lightweight design and striking cobalt-blue dial. True to Ressence’s philosophy of reimagining traditional watchmaking, it features a crownless time-setting mechanism operated via the caseback, ensuring a sleek and uninterrupted silhouette. At its heart lies the brand’s patented Ressence Orbital Convex System (ROCS), which replaces conventional hands with rotating discs that continuously orbit the dial, creating a fluid and intuitive display of time. Elegant, innovative, and understated, the Type 8 embodies Ressence’s vision of modern horology.

Condition

The watch is like new condition. The movement is running at the time of cataloging, however, it was not tested for the accuracy of time or duration of the power reserve and may need service at the buyer's discretion. Please note that Sotheby's does not guarantee the future working of the movement.


Please note that this item is final sale and not eligible for return.


  • ﻿This item ships from Hong Kong and additional duties may apply based on your location. Domestic alternatives may be available.

lots.title: F.P. Journe Chronomètre à Résonance, A platinum mechanical dual time wristwatch with power reserve, Circa 2008
lots.description:
One of the first watchmakers to incorporate the resonance effect onto a wristwatch, F.P. Journe's Resonance has gained an reputation for its eccentric design and remarkable complication. The present example is cased in platinum with a white gold dial, the movement upgraded to pink gold from the earlier brass movements.

The watch is in excellent overall condition with some minor signs of wear, The movement is running at the time of cataloging, however, it was not tested for the accuracy of time or duration of the power reserve and may need service at the buyer's discretion. Please note that Sotheby's does not guarantee the future working of the movement.

Please be advised that bands made of materials derived from endangered or otherwise protected species (i.e. alligator and crocodile) cannot be shipped outside the country of sale. If these watches are purchased outside of Hong Kong, we will replace with calf leather bands prior to shipping.

Please note that this item is final sale and not eligible for return.


  • ﻿This item ships from Hong Kong and additional duties may apply based on your location. Domestic alternatives may be available.

The movement is running at the time of cataloging, however, it was not tested for the accuracy of time or duration of the power reserve and may need service at the buyer's discretion. Please be advised that bands made of materials derived from endangered or otherwise protected species (i.e. alligator and crocodile) cannot be shipped outside the country of sale. We reserve the right to remove these bands prior to shipping.

lots.title: Cats of Wildcat Hill
lots.description:
Signed first edition first printing of Cats of Wildcat Hill with dust jacket.

  • Charis Wilson (American).
  • Edward Weston (American).
  • New York: Duell, Sloan and Pearce, 1947.
  • 90 pages.
  • Octavo.
  • Gray spine with beige titling.


Pencil autograph letter signed by Weston pasted onto the front pastedown, written on Wildcat Hill letterhead and signed by Edward Weston: "Cat Book by the Westons may be out this year via Duell, Sloan + Pearce. Cat portraits ^[a few] on display at M.M.A. New York until March 31st, part of my large retrospective. Just leaving for coast on 'Constellation,' hence haste." Recipient of letter also wrote their name in ink on the ffep and dated 1947.

lots.title: Vacheron Constantin Patrimony (Reference 81180). A white gold mechanical wristwatch. Circa 2006
lots.description:
The Patrimony line is one of the most classic and elegant offerings made by Vacheron Constantin, the oldest continuously operating watchmaker in the world. The minimalist white dial with 2-hand function makes this watch perfect for formal attire.

Condition

The watch is in very good overall condition with light surface wear throughout. The movement is running at the time of cataloging, however, it was not tested for the accuracy of time or duration of the power reserve and may need service at the buyer's discretion. Please note that Sotheby's does not guarantee the future working of the movement.


Please note that this item is final sale and not eligible for return.

lots.title: Elijah Craig Single Barrel 18 Year Old 45.0 abv 1990 (1 Bottle 75cl)
lots.description:
Elijah Craig Single Barrel 18 Year Old 45.0 abv 1990


Barrel #3978, barreled on 5-24-90, hand-selected by Fountainhead, back label scuffed and faded


1 bottle 75cl (no original packaging)


Please refer to images for levels and conditions﻿


All Lots are offered for sale in the condition they are in at the time of sale and sold “AS IS” in accordance with the Conditions of Business for Buyers. Prospective buyers should inspect each Lot to satisfy themselves as to condition. The images provided here are provided to assist you with assessing the condition of each Lot and are for guidance only; they should not be considered a replacement for inspecting the Lot in person.


Buyers are encouraged to collect their purchased Lots in person at our New York premises. Please note we do not ship spirits to any location outside of Manhattan.


﻿Please note that all spirits are sold for collection in New York or delivery within New York via specialized carriers. For delivery outside of the United States, please see Important Information for Purchases at Wine and Spirit Sales in the Conditions of Business.


Please note that for Wine and Spirits Lots, the Buyer’s Premium rate is 24% of the Hammer Price and the Overhead Premium rate is 1% of the Hammer Price. The Buyer’s Premium and Overhead Premium are subject to any applicable VAT/GST and/or sales or use tax. Please refer to the Conditions of Business for further details.

lots.title: The Complete Works Of Henry Wadsworth Longfellow
lots.description:
A finely bound collection of the works of Henry Wadsworth Longfellow.

  • Sold as a set of 11 volumes.
  • Henry Wadsworth Longfellow (American).
  • Cambridge: The Riverside Press, 1886.
  • Large paper edition.
  • With bibliographical and critical notes.
  • Engraved frontis in each volume, bound in fine three-quarter light brown morocco, ribbed gilt decorated spines, top edges gilt, others uncut, marbled pastedowns and endpapers.
